comment: Perfect base for Glacier in December. Carol kept the walk clear and the firewood stocked. Will be back.
Excellent spot for a CEU weekend. Walking distance to good coffee, quiet enough to actually study.
Great house for a family visit. Clean, comfortable, and the porch was perfect for morning coffee with Mom.
Seeley Lake is exactly the pace we needed. Cabin was spotless, trail access was immediate.
Warm, well-stocked, and close to everything we wanted in Whitefish. Two thumbs up.
Flathead Lake at sunrise is unreal. Cabin had everything we needed for a long weekend.
Hot tub after a long hike is the only way to end a day. Rena was super responsive.
Solid Helisa stay. Historic neighborhood, easy parking, decent kitchen setup.
Kootenai country is underrated. Cabin was quiet, the creek view from the back porch was worth every penny.
St. Mary entrance is the best way to start a Glacier trip. Jim had firewood in and the cabin was warm when we arrived.
Stevensville is a gem. Views of the Bitterroot Range from the kitchen window. Would stay again.
Exactly what you want in Whitefish - cozy, warm, no frills in the good sense.
Good cabin for a ski weekend. Parking was fine, beds were comfortable.
Thompson Falls was a nice surprise - very peaceful. Riverside location is legit.
Lolo is only 30 minutes from Missoula but felt like a different world. Good trail access nearby.
Rattlesnake Wilderness access from the cabin was perfect. We ran the trail before breakfast two mornings.
Unbeatable location for a winter Glacier trip. Carol was communicative and the cabin was stocked.
Seeley Lake is exactly the kind of getaway you need to recharge. Quiet, clean, great neighbors.
Hamilton homestead had a lot of character. Big kitchen was perfect for a group meal. Would go back.
Clark Fork cabin checked every box - walkable neighborhood, private hot tub, quiet at night.
